discussion_comments.comment_body: I understand that you are trying to show the latest updates to the completed challenges here. If you don't want to return data from a 204 to update the store, I suggest moving the store state modification and api request logic to a saga, send the to be deleted blocks here and handle it accordingly.

Apologies if I pointed you the wrong way, but it's fine to use a ref for this.

The tests are failing because they try to submit different challenges rapidly. So, if you use a ref then the debouncing will prevent duplicate submissions coming from a single page, but still allow playwright to quickly submit.

Great suggestion. I like to keep ref + cleanup because creating the debounced function inline can recreate it on re-renders and weaken the duplicate-click protection. The cleanup is mostly robust solution, but it keeps things safe if debounce behavior changes later.
That's fine, thanks for explaining. For future reference, please don't resolve conversations. Leaving them unresolved makes it easier for the reviewer to see what still needs their attention.
